pi_items.source: Google Play Store: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.robert.fcView&hl=en_US, APKPure database, multiple app distribution channels

AOSP notification sounds list at https://github.com/aosp-mirror/platform_frameworks_base/tree/master/data/sounds/notifications confirms multiple real Android notification sounds starting with 'C' (Cheeper, Cricket, CetiAlpha, Canopus, Capella, Castor, etc.)
AOSP notification sounds list does not contain a sound named 'Chime'. Real C-starting sounds in AOSP include: Cheeper, Cricket, CetiAlpha, Canopus, Capella, Castor, but not 'Chime'
AOSP notification sounds list does not contain a sound named 'Carbon'. No reference to 'Carbon' found in official Android system notification sounds or Pixel Sounds collections.
AOSP notification sounds list contains 'ShortCircuit' but this starts with 'S', not 'C'. No standalone 'Circuit' sound exists as a system notification sound.

privileged_info_summary: Flight Crew View (com.robert.fcView) is a verified real application used by airline crew members for schedule management, available on Google Play Store with 50,000+ downloads. The requirement to select a notification sound starting with 'C' is valid, as AOSP contains real C-starting notification sounds like Cricket, Cheeper, Canopus, Capella, and Castor. However, the specific example sounds listed in metadata ('Chime', 'Carbon', 'Circuit') do not exist in the official Android system notification sounds. Only 'ShortCircuit' is a real system sound, but it starts with 'S'. The task's core requirement (C-prefix) is achievable with actual sounds, but the claimed examples are fabricated or incorrect.
